name: Noir Temporal Cut
vietnamese_name: Toán Tử Tái Khóa Thời Gian / Noir Thời Cắt
core_equation: Ω_NTC(Kₙ, Qₙ, Nₙ, P_jn) → Kₙ₊₁ + Tₙ + Nₙ₊₁
short_definition: A conceptual operator that cuts time as a sequence of reality-lock replacements driven by unresolved possibility, Noir residue, and sustaining configuration flow.
long_definition: Noir Temporal Cut receives the current locked configuration Kₙ, the unresolved possibility field Qₙ, Noir residue Nₙ, and a sustaining flow P_jn. It evaluates whether one or more possibility candidates have enough pressure, compatibility, and residue coupling to replace the current lock. If replacement succeeds, the selected candidate becomes Kₙ₊₁, the former lock becomes temporal trace Tₙ, and unresolved components become updated residue Nₙ₊₁.

core_interpretation.time: Time is interpreted not as a passive background axis, but as the trace of repeated reality-lock replacement.
core_interpretation.future: The future is treated as unresolved possibility, not as a fixed object already existing ahead.
core_interpretation.present: The present is the configuration currently holding the reality-lock.
core_interpretation.past: The past is the previous lock after it loses current authority and becomes trace.
core_interpretation.entropy: Entropy is interpreted as residual dispersal and lost controllability, not as literal cosmic waste.
core_interpretation.noir: Noir appears simultaneously as possibility, prime seed, residue, and sustaining pressure when cut across different layers.

K_n.name: current reality-lock
K_n.description: The configuration currently treated as real, active, or dominant.

Q_n.name: possibility field
Q_n.description: Unresolved candidates that may attempt to replace the current lock.

N_n.name: Noir residue
N_n.description: Unresolved, unabsorbed, or non-locked components left outside the current lock.

P_jn.name: sustaining configuration flow
P_jn.description: The flow that maintains, outputs, and leaves residual energy or configuration pressure.

K_n_plus_1.name: next reality-lock
K_n_plus_1.description: The new configuration that wins the relock process, or the held current lock if no candidate crosses threshold.

T_n.name: past trace
T_n.description: The former lock after it is displaced into trace, memory, warning, constraint, or material.

N_n_plus_1.name: updated Noir residue
N_n_plus_1.description: The unresolved residue left after replacement or failed replacement.

entropy_reading.name: entropy interpretation
entropy_reading.description: Residual dispersal and lost controllability reading for the pulse.

reasoning_trace.name: operator trace
reasoning_trace.description: Human-readable explanation of how the operator selected, rejected, or held candidates.

runtime_pipeline.name: Receive current lock
runtime_pipeline.operation: Read Kₙ as the active configuration.

runtime_pipeline.name: Scan possibility field
runtime_pipeline.operation: Inspect Qₙ for candidates attempting to become the next lock.

runtime_pipeline.name: Measure Noir pressure
runtime_pipeline.operation: Estimate the pressure, dispersal, and unresolved content of Nₙ.

runtime_pipeline.name: Read sustaining flow
runtime_pipeline.operation: Decompose P_jn into P_lock, P_out, and P_res.

runtime_pipeline.name: Score candidates
runtime_pipeline.operation: Score each candidate by pressure, core compatibility, boundary compatibility, and residue coupling.

runtime_pipeline.name: Perform relock decision
runtime_pipeline.operation: If a candidate crosses threshold, promote it to Kₙ₊₁. Otherwise hold Kₙ.

runtime_pipeline.name: Displace former lock
runtime_pipeline.operation: Move Kₙ into Tₙ as past trace when replacement succeeds.

runtime_pipeline.name: Update residue
runtime_pipeline.operation: Return unabsorbed material to Nₙ₊₁ for future pulses.

runtime_pipeline.name: Generate XAI trace
runtime_pipeline.operation: Expose the reasoning path for explanation, audit, and comparison.

scoring_rule.interpretation: A candidate replaces the current lock only when its pressure is high enough and it can preserve or refactor the core and boundary without excessive residue explosion.

energy_mapping.P_lock: Flow used to maintain the currently locked configuration.
energy_mapping.P_out: Flow expressed outward as work, action, signal, trace, or product.
energy_mapping.P_res: Residual flow that is not lost absolutely, but leaves the main control channel and may become future input elsewhere.
energy_mapping.P_jn: Noir-like sustaining flow that can maintain, disturb, or refeed configuration locks.

treated_as: failure to relock the prior state exactly
entropy_mapping.caution: This operator does not deny thermodynamic entropy. It reinterprets entropy-like behavior for Prime-TB and Noir-style conceptual reasoning.

behaviors.name: Reality-lock replacement
behaviors.description: Promotes a possibility candidate into a new active configuration when threshold conditions are met.

behaviors.name: Past-trace formation
behaviors.description: Turns the displaced configuration into trace rather than treating it as annihilated.

behaviors.name: Residue preservation
behaviors.description: Keeps unresolved components available as pressure or material for future relocking.

behaviors.name: Entropy reinterpretation
behaviors.description: Reads entropy as dispersal and lost controllability of the current lock, not as absolute waste.

behaviors.name: XAI temporal explanation
behaviors.description: Explains why a state changed, why it did not return, and what residue remains.

examples.name: Broken cup
examples.P_jn: energy transfer through impact and material stress
examples.T_n: memory / trace of the intact cup
examples.N_n_plus_1: remaining fragments and unrecovered shape residue
examples.interpretation: The cup does not become old because a decay entity attacks it. The intact lock loses authority and the shard lock wins.

examples.name: Unchosen idea returning
examples.P_jn: attention, reading, discussion, and iterative writing
examples.K_n_plus_1: new theory organized around the returned idea
examples.T_n: old plan becomes context and trace
examples.interpretation: The future is not a fixed place; it is a candidate that becomes present when it gains enough relock pressure.

examples.name: Scientific model shift
examples.N_n: unexplained data, failed predictions, unresolved residuals
examples.P_jn: research energy, experiments, debate, and model refinement
examples.T_n: old model becomes historical trace, constraint, or approximation
examples.interpretation: Model change is a relock pulse in knowledge space.

examples.name: Biological aging as repeated relock
examples.T_n: previous body-state preserved as trace, memory, scar, or constraint
examples.N_n_plus_1: accumulated residue that lowers exact restoration ability
examples.interpretation: Aging can be read as repeated survival relocking with residue accumulation, without treating entropy as a literal waste substance.

ai_use_cases: temporal reasoning over changing knowledge states
xai_use_cases: explain why a previous state lost authority
show which candidate replaced the prior lock
separate useful output from residual dispersal

operator_boundaries: Do not use Ω_NTC to claim that physical time has been disproved.
Do not use Ω_NTC to deny thermodynamic entropy.
Do not treat Noir residue as a measured physical substance unless an empirical proxy is defined.
Do not treat future candidates as fixed destiny.
Do not treat relock score as scientific proof without benchmark, proxy definition, and controls.

requires_for_empirical_use: ablation of residue and flow variables

license_note: Add the desired license before public repository release.
author_note: Prepared as an English operator artifact for Phan Thành Trung / Trung Bờm's Operatorology and Prime Elementology corpus.
